rulings.Case Title: CHERYL SHANKS VS. WALGREEN CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 18, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTs WALGREEN CO. and JORGE BLANCO's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Ms. Shanks has pled sufficient facts to allege the seventh and eighth causes of action, specifically, that Defendants' motivating reason for their conduct was their perception of Plaintiff's race. (CACI nos. 3060, 3063. See Second Am. Complaint, 12, 26, 71, 80.) The pleadings are not uncertain. rulings.Case Title: JOY EGU VS. WALGREENS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 18, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. A court may take judicial notice of inconsistent statements by plaintiff in pleadings for purposes of a demurrer. (Owens v. King Supermarket (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 379, 384-85.) In the original complaint, Plaintiff pled that she noticed Defendant's mistake on January 16, 2012. (Complaint, Cause of Action 1 Attachment.) In the Second Amended Complaint, Plaintiff pled that she did not know about Defendant's mistake until March 22, 2012. (Second Amended Complaint, 12.) A plaintiff can explain in a subsequent complaint why the earlier statements are incorrect. If a plaintiff fails to do so, however, the statements in the earlier pleading are "read into" the complaint, and allegations inconsistent therewith are treated as sham and disregarded. (Owens, 198 Cal.App.3d at 384.) Plaintiff has not sufficiently explained why the statement in the original complaint is incorrect. Therefore, the January 16, 2012 date stated in the original complaint is "read into" the Second Amended Complaint. As such, the one-year statute of limitations for professional negligence set forth in CCP § 340.5 has run and Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is barred as untimely. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to further explain why the statement in the original complaint is incorrect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. GONZALO O MARTINEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Preclude Introduction Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 18, 2013, Line 4: Defendant GONZALO MARTINEZ Motion To Preclude Introduction Of Evidence Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same auth The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant GONZALO MARTINEZ's Motion to Preclude Introduction of Evidence is DENIED. Plaintiff CACH, LLC has complied in good faith with Defendant's demand for a Bill of Particulars. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
